title: Dhammapada
subtitle: The Path of the Dhamma
description: A collection of sayings of the Buddha in verse form, one of the most widely read and best-known Buddhist scriptures.
summary: The Dhammapada contains 423 verses organized into 26 chapters, each dealing with a specific theme. The verses reflect core Buddhist teachings on mindfulness, ethical conduct, mental discipline, and the path to enlightenment.

verses.text: Mind precedes all mental states. Mind is their chief; they are all mind-wrought. If with an impure mind a person speaks or acts, suffering follows him like the wheel that follows the foot of the ox.
verses.explanation: Mind precedes all mental states; our thoughts shape our reality. This foundational teaching from the Twin Verses declares that our entire being is formed by the mind and its thoughts.

verses.text: Mind precedes all mental states. Mind is their chief; they are all mind-wrought. If with a pure mind a person speaks or acts, happiness follows him like his never-departing shadow.
verses.explanation: Mind precedes all mental states; our thoughts shape our reality. This foundational teaching from the Twin Verses declares that our entire being is formed by the mind and its thoughts.

verses.text: He abused me, he struck me, he overpowered me, he robbed me. Those who harbor such thoughts do not still their hatred.
verses.explanation: Mind precedes all mental states; our thoughts shape our reality. This foundational teaching from the Twin Verses declares that our entire being is formed by the mind and its thoughts.

verses.text: He abused me, he struck me, he overpowered me, he robbed me. Those who do not harbor such thoughts still their hatred.
verses.explanation: Mind precedes all mental states; our thoughts shape our reality. This foundational teaching from the Twin Verses declares that our entire being is formed by the mind and its thoughts.

verses.text: Hatred is never appeased by hatred in this world. By non-hatred alone is hatred appeased. This is an eternal law.
verses.explanation: Mind precedes all mental states; our thoughts shape our reality. This foundational teaching from the Twin Verses declares that our entire being is formed by the mind and its thoughts.

verses.text: Those who think they can defeat others, and those who think they can be defeated—both are wrong. Both the victor and the vanquished experience suffering.
verses.explanation: Mind precedes all mental states; our thoughts shape our reality. This foundational teaching from the Twin Verses declares that our entire being is formed by the mind and its thoughts.

verses.text: By oneself is evil done; by oneself is one defiled. By oneself is evil left undone; by oneself is one purified. Purity and impurity depend on oneself; no one can purify another.
verses.explanation: Mind precedes all mental states; our thoughts shape our reality. This foundational teaching from the Twin Verses declares that our entire being is formed by the mind and its thoughts.

verses.text: Do not neglect your own welfare for the welfare of others, however great. Know well what is your welfare and be intent on it.
verses.explanation: Mind precedes all mental states; our thoughts shape our reality. This foundational teaching from the Twin Verses declares that our entire being is formed by the mind and its thoughts.

verses.text: Control yourself, not others. The well-controlled person enters the untroubled land.
verses.explanation: Mind precedes all mental states; our thoughts shape our reality. This foundational teaching from the Twin Verses declares that our entire being is formed by the mind and its thoughts.

verses.text: One should first establish oneself in what is proper; then only should one instruct others. Thus the wise person will not be reproached.
verses.explanation: Mind precedes all mental states; our thoughts shape our reality. This foundational teaching from the Twin Verses declares that our entire being is formed by the mind and its thoughts.

verses.text: Earnestness is the path of immortality, negligence the path of death. Those who are earnest do not die; those who are negligent are already dead.
verses.explanation: Heedfulness is the path to the immortal; heedlessness is the path to death. This teaching emphasizes that earnestness and awareness are the foundation of spiritual progress.

verses.text: Understanding this, the wise person who is earnest rejoices in earnestness and delights in it.
verses.explanation: Heedfulness is the path to the immortal; heedlessness is the path to death. This teaching emphasizes that earnestness and awareness are the foundation of spiritual progress.

verses.text: The wise and earnest person, through zeal, meditation, and self-discipline, achieves the island that no flood can overwhelm.
verses.explanation: Heedfulness is the path to the immortal; heedlessness is the path to death. This teaching emphasizes that earnestness and awareness are the foundation of spiritual progress.

verses.text: Better than a thousand empty words is one word of sense that brings peace.
verses.explanation: One meaningful verse is better than a thousand meaningless words. This teaching emphasizes quality over quantity in spiritual practice and the power of authentic wisdom.

verses.text: Better is one word of sense that brings peace than a thousand empty words.
verses.explanation: One meaningful verse is better than a thousand meaningless words. This teaching emphasizes quality over quantity in spiritual practice and the power of authentic wisdom.

verses.text: If one recites a single verse of Dhamma with understanding, it is better than reciting a hundred verses that are meaningless.
verses.explanation: One meaningful verse is better than a thousand meaningless words. This teaching emphasizes quality over quantity in spiritual practice and the power of authentic wisdom.

verses.text: Happiness comes from serving others; happiness comes from giving to others; happiness comes from doing good deeds.
verses.explanation: We live happily indeed, possessing nothing; we shall feed on joy like the radiant gods. This teaching reveals that true happiness comes from inner peace and freedom from possessiveness.

verses.text: The father and mother are happy when the son is well-behaved. So is the sage when he sees his disciples grow.
verses.explanation: We live happily indeed, possessing nothing; we shall feed on joy like the radiant gods. This teaching reveals that true happiness comes from inner peace and freedom from possessiveness.

verses.text: If one holds oneself dear, one should protect oneself well. During each of the three watches of the night, the wise person should remain alert.
verses.explanation: From affection springs grief; from affection springs fear. For one who is free from affection, there is no grief or fear. This verse teaches the danger of attachment and clinging to loved ones.

verses.text: First, establish oneself in what is proper, then instruct others. Thus the wise will not be reproached.
verses.explanation: From affection springs grief; from affection springs fear. For one who is free from affection, there is no grief or fear. This verse teaches the danger of attachment and clinging to loved ones.

verses.text: If one should love oneself well, one should protect oneself well. During each of the three watches, the wise person should remain alert.
verses.explanation: From affection springs grief; from affection springs fear. For one who is free from affection, there is no grief or fear. This verse teaches the danger of attachment and clinging to loved ones.

verses.text: He who holds anger back as a charioteer holds back a chariot, him I call a real charioteer; other charioteers only hold the reins.
verses.explanation: Let go of anger; abandon pride. Nothing exists in samsara that ultimately binds you. This teaching encourages releasing negative emotions and recognizing the impermanence of all things.

verses.text: Conquer anger by non-anger; conquer the wicked by goodness; conquer the miser by generosity; conquer the liar by truth.
verses.explanation: Let go of anger; abandon pride. Nothing exists in samsara that ultimately binds you. This teaching encourages releasing negative emotions and recognizing the impermanence of all things.

verses.text: The best of paths is the Eightfold Path; the best of truths is the Four Noble Truths; the best of mental states is detachment; the best of men is the one with eyes to see.
verses.explanation: Slowly, little by little, one should remove impurities, like a smith removes dross from silver. This verse encourages gradual self-purification and the steady removal of mental defilements.

verses.text: This is the only path, there is no other that leads to the purification of the mind. Follow this path and conquer Mara.
verses.explanation: Slowly, little by little, one should remove impurities, like a smith removes dross from silver. This verse encourages gradual self-purification and the steady removal of mental defilements.

verses.text: Following this path, you will make an end of suffering. I have taught you the path for the dissolution of sorrow.
verses.explanation: Slowly, little by little, one should remove impurities, like a smith removes dross from silver. This verse encourages gradual self-purification and the steady removal of mental defilements.

verses.text: You must yourselves strive. The Buddhas show only the way. Those who practice meditation and are saved from the fetters of death are freed.
verses.explanation: Slowly, little by little, one should remove impurities, like a smith removes dross from silver. This verse encourages gradual self-purification and the steady removal of mental defilements.

verses.text: By what attachment is one hindered? By what is one freed from attachment? Give up desire and anger, and be freed from the fetters of attachment.
verses.explanation: One is not just because one judges hastily; the wise weigh both the right and wrong. This teaching emphasizes fairness, discernment, and wisdom in judgment.

verses.text: When one has abandoned desire and anger, the fetters of attachment are destroyed. Those who overcome pride and conceit, freed from all fetters, never sorrow.
verses.explanation: One is not just because one judges hastily; the wise weigh both the right and wrong. This teaching emphasizes fairness, discernment, and wisdom in judgment.

verses.text: Not in the sky, not in the midst of the ocean, nor in a mountain cave, is one free from the consequences of evil deeds.
verses.explanation: The best of paths is the Eightfold Path; the best of truths is the Four Noble Truths. This verse points to the heart of Buddhist teachings and the way to liberation.

verses.text: Not in the sky, not in the midst of the ocean, nor in a mountain cave, is one free from suffering, except in the shelter of the Noble Ones.
verses.explanation: The best of paths is the Eightfold Path; the best of truths is the Four Noble Truths. This verse points to the heart of Buddhist teachings and the way to liberation.

verses.text: Not by tonsure is one a Brahmin. If one is free from anger and fault, tranquil, self-controlled, and pure—one is a Brahmin.
verses.explanation: If you see a wise man who points out faults, follow him as one who shows hidden treasures. This teaching encourages valuing constructive criticism and learning from those who are wise.

verses.text: By what should one be called a Brahmin? By what should one be called a monk? By what should one be called a renouncer?
verses.explanation: If you see a wise man who points out faults, follow him as one who shows hidden treasures. This teaching encourages valuing constructive criticism and learning from those who are wise.

verses.text: He who has cut off all ill will and enmity, who is freed from anger, who is modest, who is free from attachment—that one I call a Brahmin.
verses.explanation: If you see a wise man who points out faults, follow him as one who shows hidden treasures. This teaching encourages valuing constructive criticism and learning from those who are wise.

verses.text: He who has subdued himself, who is self-controlled, who has mastered himself, who has attained the highest self-mastery—that one I call a Brahmin.
verses.explanation: If you see a wise man who points out faults, follow him as one who shows hidden treasures. This teaching encourages valuing constructive criticism and learning from those who are wise.

verses.text: He whose senses are tamed, like horses well-trained by a charioteer; he who has discarded pride, is free from impurities—that one I call a sage.
verses.explanation: The heedless who live in sin fall into hell; the vigilant who live rightly reach nirvana. This verse contrasts the destinies of the heedless and the vigilant in clear terms.

verses.text: Such a sage, like a lake, is deep, difficult to fathom, and pure. Upon hearing the Dhamma, one becomes serene.
verses.explanation: The heedless who live in sin fall into hell; the vigilant who live rightly reach nirvana. This verse contrasts the destinies of the heedless and the vigilant in clear terms.

verses.text: He who destroys anger by non-anger, the wicked by good, the miser by generosity, the liar by truth—that one I call a sage.
verses.explanation: Like a war elephant in battle, I will endure the arrows of harsh words. This teaching encourages patience, endurance, and equanimity in the face of adversity.

verses.text: One who speaks truth, who does not yield to anger, who gives even when asked for little—that one I call a sage.
verses.explanation: Like a war elephant in battle, I will endure the arrows of harsh words. This teaching encourages patience, endurance, and equanimity in the face of adversity.

verses.text: He who has cut off affection for son and cattle, who has renounced all sensual pleasures—who is free from the conceit 'I am'—him I call a true Brahmin.
verses.explanation: The craving of one who is addicted to heedlessness grows like a creeper; he jumps from life to life. This verse warns of the endless cycle of desire and the suffering it perpetuates.

verses.text: He who has cut off all fetters, who is free from anger, who has transcended entanglements, who has uprooted attachment—that one I call a true Brahmin.
verses.explanation: The craving of one who is addicted to heedlessness grows like a creeper; he jumps from life to life. This verse warns of the endless cycle of desire and the suffering it perpetuates.

verses.text: He who has discarded anger and conceit, who has overcome all fetters, who has uprooted attachment, who is free from the conceit 'I am'—that one I call a true Brahmin.
verses.explanation: The craving of one who is addicted to heedlessness grows like a creeper; he jumps from life to life. This verse warns of the endless cycle of desire and the suffering it perpetuates.

verses.text: He who has no anger, who is devout, who is pure, who is free from passion, who has conquered himself, who is worthy of offerings—that one I call a true Brahmin.
verses.explanation: The craving of one who is addicted to heedlessness grows like a creeper; he jumps from life to life. This verse warns of the endless cycle of desire and the suffering it perpetuates.

verses.text: He who has subdued himself, who is self-controlled, who has mastered himself, who has attained the highest self-mastery—that one I call a true Brahmin.
verses.explanation: The craving of one who is addicted to heedlessness grows like a creeper; he jumps from life to life. This verse warns of the endless cycle of desire and the suffering it perpetuates.

verses.text: He who is not angry, who is devout, who is pure, who is free from passion, who is free from pride—that one I call a true Brahmin.
verses.explanation: The craving of one who is addicted to heedlessness grows like a creeper; he jumps from life to life. This verse warns of the endless cycle of desire and the suffering it perpetuates.

verses.text: The mind is hard to restrain, swift it flies wherever it likes: to control the mind is good; a controlled mind brings happiness.
verses.explanation: The monk who has mastered solitude and calmed his mind perceives the truth. This teaching extols the virtues of simplicity, meditation, and the contemplative life.

verses.text: The mind is very hard to see, subtle it flies wherever it likes: the sage guards the mind; a guarded mind brings happiness.
verses.explanation: The monk who has mastered solitude and calmed his mind perceives the truth. This teaching extols the virtues of simplicity, meditation, and the contemplative life.

verses.text: The mind is volatile; it flies and dallies wherever it likes: a tamed mind brings happiness.
verses.explanation: The monk who has mastered solitude and calmed his mind perceives the truth. This teaching extols the virtues of simplicity, meditation, and the contemplative life.

verses.text: Whatever an enemy may do to an enemy, or a hater to a hater, a wrongly directed mind will do greater harm.
verses.explanation: The monk who has mastered solitude and calmed his mind perceives the truth. This teaching extols the virtues of simplicity, meditation, and the contemplative life.

verses.text: There is no fires like passion, no snare like hatred, no entanglement like delusion, no river like craving.
verses.explanation: The monk who has mastered solitude and calmed his mind perceives the truth. This teaching extols the virtues of simplicity, meditation, and the contemplative life.

verses.text: The mind is the forerunner of all actions. All deeds are led by mind, created by mind. If one speaks or acts with a corrupt mind, suffering follows, as the wheel follows the hoof of the ox.
verses.explanation: The monk who has mastered solitude and calmed his mind perceives the truth. This teaching extols the virtues of simplicity, meditation, and the contemplative life.

verses.text: Mind is the forerunner of all actions. All deeds are led by mind, created by mind. If one speaks or acts with a serene mind, happiness follows, as a shadow that never departs.
verses.explanation: The monk who has mastered solitude and calmed his mind perceives the truth. This teaching extols the virtues of simplicity, meditation, and the contemplative life.

verses.text: The fragrance of virtue travels against the wind and pervades all directions. The fragrance of virtue pervades heaven and earth.
verses.explanation: One is not a brahmana by birth but by the eradication of all bonds. This verse emphasizes that spiritual attainment transcends birth, caste, and social status.

verses.text: The scent of flowers does not travel against the wind; but the fragrance of virtue travels against the wind; the good person pervades every direction.
verses.explanation: One is not a brahmana by birth but by the eradication of all bonds. This verse emphasizes that spiritual attainment transcends birth, caste, and social status.

verses.text: As on a heap of refuse thrown upon the highway, a lily may grow, sweet-smelling and delightful, so among blind, erring mortals, the disciple of the truly awakened one shines with wisdom.
verses.explanation: One is not a brahmana by birth but by the eradication of all bonds. This verse emphasizes that spiritual attainment transcends birth, caste, and social status.

verses.text: If many years a person live in evil, but overcomes evil, that person illuminates the world, as the moon freed from clouds.
verses.explanation: One is not a brahmana by birth but by the eradication of all bonds. This verse emphasizes that spiritual attainment transcends birth, caste, and social status.

verses.text: If one man conquer in battle a thousand times a thousand men, and if another conquer himself, he is the greatest of conquerors.
verses.explanation: One is not a brahmana by birth but by the eradication of all bonds. This verse emphasizes that spiritual attainment transcends birth, caste, and social status.

verses.text: He who lives only for pleasures, without self-control and mindfulness, wasting his opportunities, that idle person envies the strenuous.
verses.explanation: Evil deeds return to the fool like fine dust thrown against the wind. This teaching warns of the karmic consequences of unwholesome actions.

verses.text: Do not follow the low path, do not live in negligence, do not follow false doctrine, do not be attached to the world.
verses.explanation: Evil deeds return to the fool like fine dust thrown against the wind. This teaching warns of the karmic consequences of unwholesome actions.

verses.text: There is no disease like craving, no pain like mental formation, no happiness like the Dhamma, no suffering greater than the aggregates.
verses.explanation: Evil deeds return to the fool like fine dust thrown against the wind. This teaching warns of the karmic consequences of unwholesome actions.

verses.text: He who holds nothing dear and nothing hateful, he who has ceased former hatred and is not attached to new ones—he attains freedom.
verses.explanation: What is laughter, what is joy, when the world is ever burning? Shrouded by darkness, do you not seek the light? This verse urges us to recognize impermanence and seek spiritual awakening.

verses.text: He who loves nothing, hates nothing; there is no fetter for him whose passion is extinct. He who is free from desire and craving finds peace.
verses.explanation: What is laughter, what is joy, when the world is ever burning? Shrouded by darkness, do you not seek the light? This verse urges us to recognize impermanence and seek spiritual awakening.

verses.text: He who loves nothing, hates nothing; there is no fetter for him whose passion is extinct. He who is free from greed finds peace.
verses.explanation: What is laughter, what is joy, when the world is ever burning? Shrouded by darkness, do you not seek the light? This verse urges us to recognize impermanence and seek spiritual awakening.

verses.text: If you love a son, you will be troubled by the world. If you love nothing, you will have no trouble.
verses.explanation: What is laughter, what is joy, when the world is ever burning? Shrouded by darkness, do you not seek the light? This verse urges us to recognize impermanence and seek spiritual awakening.

verses.text: When lust is conquered, the fetters are broken, all sufferings cease, all chains fall away.
verses.explanation: What is laughter, what is joy, when the world is ever burning? Shrouded by darkness, do you not seek the light? This verse urges us to recognize impermanence and seek spiritual awakening.

verses.text: He who holds anger back, as one would restrain a chariot, is called a real driver; others merely hold the reins.
verses.explanation: The self is the master of the self; what other master could there be? This teaching emphasizes personal responsibility and self-reliance on the spiritual path.

verses.text: A true Brahmin who conquers anger is free from arrogance, sloth, and deceit. He is near the state of Nirvana.
verses.explanation: The self is the master of the self; what other master could there be? This teaching emphasizes personal responsibility and self-reliance on the spiritual path.

verses.text: Overcome anger by non-anger, evil by good, stinginess by generosity, falsehood by truth.
verses.explanation: The self is the master of the self; what other master could there be? This teaching emphasizes personal responsibility and self-reliance on the spiritual path.

verses.text: Be truthful; do not yield to anger; give to those who ask; by these three virtues you go near the gods.
verses.explanation: The self is the master of the self; what other master could there be? This teaching emphasizes personal responsibility and self-reliance on the spiritual path.

verses.text: All fear the staff; all fear death. Putting oneself in the place of others, one should neither kill nor cause to kill.
verses.explanation: The path to the unconditioned is through mindfulness and effort. This teaching reinforces that liberation is achieved through sustained awareness and diligent practice.

verses.text: He who seeks happiness by inflicting suffering on others will not find happiness, for happiness is only found in non-violence.
verses.explanation: The path to the unconditioned is through mindfulness and effort. This teaching reinforces that liberation is achieved through sustained awareness and diligent practice.

verses.text: None should ever harm another, nor should one desire harm from another. A person who desires happiness should not harm any being.
verses.explanation: The path to the unconditioned is through mindfulness and effort. This teaching reinforces that liberation is achieved through sustained awareness and diligent practice.

verses.text: The righteous should avoid the company of the wicked. The righteous should avoid the company of the wicked.
verses.explanation: The path to the unconditioned is through mindfulness and effort. This teaching reinforces that liberation is achieved through sustained awareness and diligent practice.

verses.text: If a person is easily stirred to anger, he looks wretched. His body suffers; his mind is disturbed.
verses.explanation: The path to the unconditioned is through mindfulness and effort. This teaching reinforces that liberation is achieved through sustained awareness and diligent practice.

verses.text: Whatever a hater does to a hater, or an enemy to an enemy, a wrongly directed mind will do greater harm.
verses.explanation: The path to the unconditioned is through mindfulness and effort. This teaching reinforces that liberation is achieved through sustained awareness and diligent practice.

verses.text: Youth is not lasting; it is but the time of a meeting of the senses. Seeing that this is so, a wise person should refrain from sense pleasures.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: Youth passes, beauty passes, they are but the time of a meeting of the senses. Seeing that this is so, a wise person should refrain from sense pleasures.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: He who is like a withered leaf, which has come from the tree of birth—his time has come, his life is spent. Seeing this, a wise person should refrain from sense pleasures.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: He who is like a withered leaf, which has come from the tree of birth—his time has come, his life is spent. Seeing this, a wise person should restrain himself.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: One is one's own refuge, who else could be the refuge? One who has mastered oneself is an island of refuge.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: Well have you done what should be done. You are the master of your own mind. Exert yourself, for self-control is the way to heaven.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: A person is not an aristocrat by birth but by deed. One who speaks truth, who acts rightly, who harms no one—that one is a true Brahmin.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: The wise person exerts himself more than the idle. The wise person is the true refuge of himself.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: If one conquers oneself, one is greater than those who conquer a thousand men in battle.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: He who conquers oneself is the best of conquerors. One who masters oneself is always serene.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: Walk as if you are kissing the earth with your feet. Be steadfast, devoted, firm in virtue, gentle in spirit, humble.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: Let the wise person guard oneself as a trader guards a valuable cargo. Let the wise person abandon evil, as one discards straw for gold.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: The evil done by oneself falls upon the fool as dust falls on a fresh wind.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

chapters.title: Buddha Vagga (The Awakened One / The Buddha)

verses.text: He whose senses are under control, like well-trained horses, free from pride, free from defilement—he is held in high regard even by the gods.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: Like an elephant in battle, enduring the arrow shot from a bow, I will endure abuse, for most people are undisciplined.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: The awakened one has no interest in sense pleasures, and his joy is in the destruction of craving. He uproots attachment and cuts off the fetter of existence.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: Let a person live with the righteous, let him associate with the good. By association with the good, the good become good.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: He who lives with the good, and who delights in the teaching of the saints, gains wisdom, and his suffering ends.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: If you associate with a foolish person, you will suffer all your life. association with a fool is painful, as with an enemy.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: Follow the good, follow the wise. The wise and the good are happy even in death.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.

verses.text: The wise person should practice virtue, concentration, and wisdom, as the wise person practices restraint in the world.
verses.explanation: This verse from the Dhammapada continues the Buddha's guidance on discipline, wisdom, and the fruits of the holy life, urging the seeker onward.
